computeModel.description: The OCI model compute model used when you create or clone an instance: ECPU or OCPU. An ECPU is an abstracted measure of compute resources. ECPUs are based on the number of cores elastically allocated from a pool of compute and storage servers. An OCPU is a legacy physical measure of compute resources. OCPUs are based on the physical core of a processor with hyper-threading enabled.

nextToken.description: The token to include in another request to get the next page of items. This value is null when there are no more items to return.

diskRedundancy.description: The type of redundancy configured for the VM cluster. NORMAL is 2-way redundancy. HIGH is 3-way redundancy.

hostname.description: The host name for the VM cluster.

systemVersion.description: The operating system version of the image chosen for the VM cluster.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the VM cluster was created.

timeZone.description: The time zone of the VM cluster.

vipIds.description: The virtual IP (VIP) addresses that are associated with the VM cluster. Oracle's Cluster Ready Services (CRS) creates and maintains one VIP address for each node in the VM cluster to enable failover. If one node fails, the VIP is reassigned to another active node in the cluster.

ECPUs are based on the number of cores elastically allocated from a pool of compute and storage servers. An OCPU is a legacy physical measure of compute resources. OCPUs are based on the physical core of a processor with hyper-threading enabled.

availableStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of available storage, in gigabytes (GB), for the Exadata infrastructure.

availabilityZone.description: The name of the Availability Zone (AZ) where the Exadata infrastructure is located.

availabilityZoneId.description: The AZ ID of the AZ where the Exadata infrastructure is located.

computeCount.description: The number of database servers for the Exadata infrastructure.

cpuCount.description: The total number of CPU cores that are allocated to the Exadata infrastructure.

customerContactsToSendToOCI.description: The email addresses of contacts to receive notification from Oracle about maintenance updates for the Exadata infrastructure.

dataStorageSizeInTBs.description: The size of the Exadata infrastructure's data disk group, in terabytes (TB).

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The size of the Exadata infrastructure's local node storage, in gigabytes (GB).

dbServerVersion.description: The software version of the database servers (dom0) in the Exadata infrastructure.

lastMaintenanceRunId.description: The Oracle Cloud Identifier (OCID) of the last maintenance run for the Exadata infrastructure.

maintenanceWindow.description: The scheduling details for the maintenance window. Patching and system updates take place during the maintenance window.

customActionTimeoutInMins.description: The custom action timeout in minutes for the maintenance window.

daysOfWeek.description: The days of the week when maintenance can be performed.

hoursOfDay.description: The hours of the day when maintenance can be performed.

isCustomActionTimeoutEnabled.description: Indicates whether custom action timeout is enabled for the maintenance window.

leadTimeInWeeks.description: The lead time in weeks before the maintenance window.

months.description: The months when maintenance can be performed.

patchingMode.description: The patching mode for the maintenance window.

preference.description: The preference for the maintenance window scheduling.

skipRu.description: Indicates whether to skip release updates during maintenance.

weeksOfMonth.description: The weeks of the month when maintenance can be performed.

maxCpuCount.description: The total number of CPU cores available on the Exadata infrastructure.

maxDataStorageInTBs.description: The total amount of data disk group storage, in terabytes (TB), that's available on the Exadata infrastructure.

maxDbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The total amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's available on the Exadata infrastructure.

maxMemoryInGBs.description: The total amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that's available on the Exadata infrastructure.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated on the Exadata infrastructure.

monthlyDbServerVersion.description: The monthly software version of the database servers installed on the Exadata infrastructure.

monthlyStorageServerVersion.description: The monthly software version of the storage servers installed on the Exadata infrastructure.

nextMaintenanceRunId.description: The OCID of the next maintenance run for the Exadata infrastructure.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the Exadata infrastructure.

ociUrl.description: The HTTPS link to the Exadata infrastructure in OCI.

shape.description: The model name of the Exadata infrastructure.

storageCount.description: The number of storage servers that are activated for the Exadata infrastructure.

storageServerVersion.description: The software version of the storage servers on the Exadata infrastructure.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the Exadata infrastructure was created.

totalStorageSizeInGBs.description: The total amount of storage, in gigabytes (GB), on the the Exadata infrastructure.

percentProgress.description: The amount of progress made on the current operation on the Exadata infrastructure, expressed as a percentage.

databaseServerType.description: The database server model type of the Exadata infrastructure. For the list of valid model names, use the ListDbSystemShapes operation.

storageServerType.description: The storage server model type of the Exadata infrastructure. For the list of valid model names, use the ListDbSystemShapes operation.

computeModel.description: The OCI model compute model used when you create or clone an instance: ECPU or OCPU. An ECPU is an abstracted measure of compute resources. ECPUs are based on the number of cores elastically allocated from a pool of compute and storage servers. An OCPU is a legacy physical measure of compute resources. OCPUs are based on the physical core of a processor with hyper-threading enabled.

cloudExadataInfrastructureUnallocatedRes.description: Details about the unallocated resources in the specified Cloud Exadata infrastructure.

cloudAutonomousVmClusters.description: A list of Autonomous VM clusters associated with this Cloud Exadata Infrastructure.

cloudExadataInfrastructureDisplayName.description: The display name of the Cloud Exadata infrastructure.

exadataStorageInTBs.description: The amount of unallocated Exadata storage available, in terabytes (TB).

cloudExadataInfrastructureId.description: The unique identifier of the Cloud Exadata infrastructure.

localStorageInGBs.description: The amount of unallocated local storage available, in gigabytes (GB).

memoryInGBs.description: The amount of unallocated memory available, in gigabytes (GB).

ocpus.description: The number of unallocated Oracle CPU Units (OCPUs) available.

cloudVmClusterId.description: The unique identifier of the VM cluster.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name for the VM cluster.

status.description: The current status of the VM cluster.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the VM cluster.

cloudVmClusterArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the VM cluster.

cloudExadataInfrastructureId.description: The unique identifier of the Exadata infrastructure that this VM cluster belongs to.

clusterName.description: The name of the Grid Infrastructure (GI) cluster.

cpuCoreCount.description: The number of CPU cores enabled on the VM cluster.

dataCollectionOptions.description: The set of diagnostic collection options enabled for the VM cluster.

isDiagnosticsEventsEnabled.description: Indicates whether diagnostic collection is enabled for the VM cluster.

isHealthMonitoringEnabled.description: Indicates whether health monitoring is enabled for the VM cluster.

isIncidentLogsEnabled.description: Indicates whether incident logs are enabled for the cloud VM cluster.

dataStorageSizeInTBs.description: The size of the data disk group, in terabytes (TB), that's allocated for the VM cluster.

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated for the VM cluster.

dbServers.description: The list of database servers for the VM cluster.

diskRedundancy.description: The type of redundancy configured for the VM cluster. NORMAL is 2-way redundancy. HIGH is 3-way redundancy.

giVersion.description: The software version of the Oracle Grid Infrastructure (GI) for the VM cluster.

hostname.description: The host name for the VM cluster.

iormConfigCache.description: The ExadataIormConfig cache details for the VM cluster.

dbPlans.description: An array of IORM settings for all the database in the Exadata DB system.

lifecycleState.description: The current state of IORM configuration for the Exadata DB system.

objective.description: The current value for the IORM objective. The default is AUTO.

isLocalBackupEnabled.description: Indicates whether database backups to local Exadata storage is enabled for the VM cluster.

isSparseDiskgroupEnabled.description: Indicates whether the VM cluster is configured with a sparse disk group.

lastUpdateHistoryEntryId.description: The Oracle Cloud ID (OCID) of the last maintenance update history entry.

licenseModel.description: The Oracle license model applied to the VM cluster.

listenerPort.description: The port number configured for the listener on the VM cluster.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated for the VM cluster.

nodeCount.description: The number of nodes in the VM cluster.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the VM cluster.

ociUrl.description: The HTTPS link to the VM cluster in OCI.

scanDnsName.description: The FQDN of the DNS record for the Single Client Access Name (SCAN) IP addresses that are associated with the VM cluster.

scanDnsRecordId.description: The OCID of the DNS record for the SCAN IP addresses that are associated with the VM cluster.

scanIpIds.description: The OCID of the SCAN IP addresses that are associated with the VM cluster.

shape.description: The hardware model name of the Exadata infrastructure that's running the VM cluster.

sshPublicKeys.description: The public key portion of one or more key pairs used for SSH access to the VM cluster.

storageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated to the VM cluster.

systemVersion.description: The operating system version of the image chosen for the VM cluster.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the VM cluster was created.

timeZone.description: The time zone of the VM cluster.

vipIds.description: The virtual IP (VIP) addresses that are associated with the VM cluster. Oracle's Cluster Ready Services (CRS) creates and maintains one VIP address for each node in the VM cluster to enable failover. If one node fails, the VIP is reassigned to another active node in the cluster.

odbNetworkId.description: The unique identifier of the ODB network for the VM cluster.

percentProgress.description: The amount of progress made on the current operation on the VM cluster, expressed as a percentage.

computeModel.description: The OCI model compute model used when you create or clone an instance: ECPU or OCPU. An ECPU is an abstracted measure of compute resources. ECPUs are based on the number of cores elastically allocated from a pool of compute and storage servers. An OCPU is a legacy physical measure of compute resources. OCPUs are based on the physical core of a processor with hyper-threading enabled.

iamRoles.description: The Amazon Web Services Identity and Access Management (IAM) service roles associated with the VM cluster.

dbNodeId.description: The unique identifier of the DB node.

dbNodeArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the DB node.

status.description: The current status of the DB node.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the DB node.

backupIpId.description: The Oracle Cloud ID (OCID) of the backup IP address that's associated with the DB node.

backupVnic2Id.description: The OCID of the second backup VNIC.

cpuCoreCount.description: Number of CPU cores enabled on the DB node.

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GBs), that's allocated on the DB node.

dbServerId.description: The unique identifier of the Db server that is associated with the DB node.

faultDomain.description: The name of the fault domain the instance is contained in.

hostIpId.description: The OCID of the host IP address that's associated with the DB node.

hostname.description: The host name for the DB node.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the DB node.

maintenanceType.description: The type of database node maintenance. Either VMDB_REBOOT_MIGRATION or EXADBXS_REBOOT_MIGRATION.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The allocated memory in GBs on the DB node.

softwareStorageSizeInGB.description: The size (in GB) of the block storage volume allocation for the DB system.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the DB node was created.

timeMaintenanceWindowEnd.description: End date and time of maintenance window.

timeMaintenanceWindowStart.description: Start date and time of maintenance window.

totalCpuCoreCount.description: The total number of CPU cores reserved on the DB node.

privateIpAddress.description: The private IP address assigned to the DB node.

floatingIpAddress.description: The floating IP address assigned to the DB node.

dbServer.description: The details of the requested database server.

dbServerId.description: The unique identifier for the database server.

status.description: The current status of the database server.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the current status of the database server.

cpuCoreCount.description: The number of CPU cores enabled on the database server.

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The allocated local node storage in GBs on the database server.

dbServerPatchingDetails.description: The scheduling details for the quarterly maintenance window. Patching and system updates take place during the maintenance window.

estimatedPatchDuration.description: Estimated time, in minutes, to patch one database server.

patchingStatus.description: The status of the patching operation. Possible values are SCHEDULED, MAINTENANCE_IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, and COMPLETE.

timePatchingEnded.description: The time when the patching operation ended.

timePatchingStarted.description: The time when the patching operation started.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name of the database server.

exadataInfrastructureId.description: The ID of the Exadata infrastructure the database server belongs to.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the database server.

maxCpuCount.description: The total number of CPU cores available.

maxDbNodeStorageInGBs.description: The total local node storage available in GBs.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The allocated memory in GBs on the database server.

shape.description: The shape of the database server. The shape determines the amount of CPU, storage, and memory resources available.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the database server was created.

vmClusterIds.description: The OCID of the VM clusters that are associated with the database server.

computeModel.description: The compute model of the database server (ECPU or OCPU).

autonomousVmClusterIds.description: The list of identifiers for the Autonomous VM clusters associated with this database server.

autonomousVirtualMachineIds.description: The list of unique identifiers for the Autonomous VMs associated with this database server.

odbNetworkId.description: The unique identifier of the ODB network.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name of the ODB network.

status.description: The current status of the ODB network.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the current status of the ODB network.

odbNetworkArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the ODB network.

availabilityZone.description: The Amazon Web Services Availability Zone (AZ) where the ODB network is located.

availabilityZoneId.description: The AZ ID of the AZ where the ODB network is located.

clientSubnetCidr.description: The CIDR range of the client subnet in the ODB network.

backupSubnetCidr.description: The CIDR range of the backup subnet in the ODB network.

customDomainName.description: The domain name for the resources in the ODB network.

defaultDnsPrefix.description: The DNS prefix to the default DNS domain name. The default DNS domain name is oraclevcn.com.

peeredCidrs.description: The list of CIDR ranges from the peered VPC that are allowed access to the ODB network.

ociNetworkAnchorId.description: The unique identifier of the OCI network anchor for the ODB network.

ociNetworkAnchorUrl.description: The URL of the OCI network anchor for the ODB network.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor that's associated with the ODB network.

ociVcnId.description: The Oracle Cloud ID (OCID) for the Virtual Cloud Network (VCN) that's associated with the ODB network.

ociVcnUrl.description: The URL for the VCN that's associated with the ODB network.

ociDnsForwardingConfigs.description: The DNS resolver endpoint in OCI for forwarding DNS queries for the ociPrivateZone domain.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the ODB network was created.

percentProgress.description: The amount of progress made on the current operation on the ODB network, expressed as a percentage.

managedServices.description: The managed services configuration for the ODB network.

serviceNetworkArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the service network.

resourceGatewayArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource gateway.

managedServicesIpv4Cidrs.description: The IPv4 CIDR blocks for the managed services.

managedS3BackupAccess.description: The managed Amazon S3 backup access configuration.

status.description: The status of the managed Amazon S3 backup access.

ipv4Addresses.description: The IPv4 addresses for the managed Amazon S3 backup access.

status.description: The status of the Zero-ETL access.

cidr.description: The CIDR block for the Zero-ETL access.

status.description: The status of the Amazon S3 access.

ipv4Addresses.description: The IPv4 addresses for the Amazon S3 access.

domainName.description: The domain name for the Amazon S3 access.

s3PolicyDocument.description: The endpoint policy for the Amazon S3 access.

stsAccess.description: The Amazon Web Services Security Token Service (STS) access configuration for managed services.

status.description: The current status of the STS access configuration.

ipv4Addresses.description: The IPv4 addresses allowed for STS access.

domainName.description: The domain name for STS access configuration.

stsPolicyDocument.description: The STS policy document that defines permissions for token service usage.

kmsAccess.description: The Amazon Web Services Key Management Service (KMS) access configuration for managed services.

status.description: The current status of the KMS access configuration.

ipv4Addresses.description: The IPv4 addresses allowed for KMS access.

domainName.description: The domain name for KMS access configuration.

kmsPolicyDocument.description: The KMS policy document that defines permissions for key usage.

crossRegionS3RestoreSourcesAccess.description: The access configuration for the cross-Region Amazon S3 database restore source.

odbPeeringConnection.description: A peering connection between an ODB network and either another ODB network or a customer-owned VPC.

odbPeeringConnectionId.description: The unique identifier of the ODB peering connection. A sample ID is odbpcx-abcdefgh12345678.

displayName.description: The display name of the ODB peering connection.

status.description: The status of the ODB peering connection.

statusReason.description: The reason for the current status of the ODB peering connection.

odbPeeringConnectionArn.description:
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the ODB peering connection.

Example: arn:aws:odb:us-east-1:123456789012:odb-peering-connection/odbpcx-abcdefgh12345678

odbNetworkArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the ODB network that initiated the peering connection.

peerNetworkArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the peer network.

odbPeeringConnectionType.description:
The type of the ODB peering connection.

Valid Values: ODB-VPC | ODB-ODB

peerNetworkCidrs.description: The CIDR blocks associated with the peering connection. These CIDR blocks define the IP address ranges that can communicate through the peering connection.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the ODB peering connection was created.

percentProgress.description: The percentage progress of the ODB peering connection creation or deletion.

nextToken.description: The pagination token from which to continue listing.

autonomousVirtualMachines.description: The list of Autonomous VMs in the specified Autonomous VM cluster.

autonomousVirtualMachineId.description: The unique identifier of the Autonomous VM.

status.description: The current status of the Autonomous VM.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the current status of the Autonomous VM, if applicable.

dbServerId.description: The unique identifier of the database server hosting this Autonomous VM.

dbServerDisplayName.description: The display name of the database server hosting this Autonomous VM.

cpuCoreCount.description: The number of CPU cores allocated to this Autonomous VM.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The amount of memory allocated to this Autonomous VM, in gigabytes (GB).

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of storage allocated to this Autonomous Virtual Machine, in gigabytes (GB).

clientIpAddress.description: The IP address used by clients to connect to this Autonomous VM.

cloudAutonomousVmClusterId.description: The unique identifier of the Autonomous VM cluster containing this Autonomous VM.

ocid.description: The Oracle Cloud Identifier (OCID) of the Autonomous VM.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) resource anchor associated with this Autonomous VM.

nextToken.description: The token to include in another request to get the next page of items. This value is null when there are no more items to return.

dbNodes.description: The list of DB nodes along with their properties.

dbNodeId.description: The unique identifier of the DB node.

dbNodeArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the DB node.

status.description: The current status of the DB node.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the DB node.

backupIpId.description: The Oracle Cloud ID (OCID) of the backup IP address that's associated with the DB node.

backupVnic2Id.description: The OCID of the second backup virtual network interface card (VNIC) for the DB node.

backupVnicId.description: The OCID of the backup VNIC for the DB node.

cpuCoreCount.description: The number of CPU cores enabled on the DB node.

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated on the DB node.

dbServerId.description: The unique identifier of the database server that's associated with the DB node.

faultDomain.description: The name of the fault domain where the DB node is located.

hostIpId.description: The OCID of the host IP address that's associated with the DB node.

hostname.description: The host name for the DB node.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the DB node.

maintenanceType.description: The type of maintenance the DB node.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that allocated on the DB node.

softwareStorageSizeInGB.description: The size of the block storage volume, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated for the DB system. This attribute applies only for virtual machine DB systems.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the DB node was created.

timeMaintenanceWindowEnd.description: The end date and time of the maintenance window.

timeMaintenanceWindowStart.description: The start date and time of the maintenance window.

totalCpuCoreCount.description: The total number of CPU cores reserved on the DB node.

nextToken.description: The token to include in another request to get the next page of items. This value is null when there are no more items to return.

dbServers.description: The list of database servers along with their properties.

dbServerId.description: The unique identifier of the database server.

status.description: The current status of the database server.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the database server.

cpuCoreCount.description: The number of CPU cores enabled on the database server.

dbNodeStorageSizeInGBs.description: The amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated on the database server.

dbServerPatchingDetails.description: The scheduling details for the quarterly maintenance window. Patching and system updates take place during the maintenance window.

estimatedPatchDuration.description: Estimated time, in minutes, to patch one database server.

patchingStatus.description: The status of the patching operation. Possible values are SCHEDULED, MAINTENANCE_IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, and COMPLETE.

timePatchingEnded.description: The time when the patching operation ended.

timePatchingStarted.description: The time when the patching operation started.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name of the database server. The name doesn't need to be unique.

exadataInfrastructureId.description: The ID of the Exadata infrastructure that hosts the database server.

ociResourceAnchorName.description: The name of the OCI resource anchor for the database server.

maxCpuCount.description: The total number of CPU cores available on the database server.

maxDbNodeStorageInGBs.description: The total amount of local node storage, in gigabytes (GB), that's available on the database server.

maxMemoryInGBs.description: The total amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that's available on the database server.

memorySizeInGBs.description: The amount of memory, in gigabytes (GB), that's allocated on the database server.

shape.description: The hardware system model of the Exadata infrastructure that the database server is hosted on. The shape determines the amount of CPU, storage, and memory resources available.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the database server was created.

vmClusterIds.description: The IDs of the VM clusters that are associated with the database server.

computeModel.description: The OCI model compute model used when you create or clone an instance: ECPU or OCPU. An ECPU is an abstracted measure of compute resources. ECPUs are based on the number of cores elastically allocated from a pool of compute and storage servers. An OCPU is a legacy physical measure of compute resources. OCPUs are based on the physical core of a processor with hyper-threading enabled.

autonomousVmClusterIds.description: A list of identifiers for the Autonomous VM clusters.

autonomousVirtualMachineIds.description: A list of unique identifiers for the Autonomous VMs.

nextToken.description: The token to include in another request to get the next page of items. This value is null when there are no more items to return.

systemVersions.description: The Exadata system versions that are compatible with the specified Exadata shape and GI version.

tags.description: The list of tags applied to the resource.

dbNodeId.description: The unique identifier of the DB node that was rebooted.

status.description: The current status of the DB node after the reboot operation.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the DB node after the reboot operation.

dbNodeId.description: The unique identifier of the DB node that was started.

status.description: The current status of the DB node after the start operation.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the DB node after the start operation.

dbNodeId.description: The unique identifier of the DB node that was stopped.

status.description: The current status of the DB node after the stop operation.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the DB node after the stop operation.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name of the updated Exadata infrastructure.

status.description: The current status of the Exadata infrastructure after the update operation.

statusReason.description: Additional information about the status of the Exadata infrastructure after the update operation.

cloudExadataInfrastructureId.description: The unique identifier of the updated Exadata infrastructure.

displayName.description: The user-friendly name of the ODB network.

status.description: The current status of the ODB network.

odbNetworkId.description: The unique identifier of the ODB network.
